stem: The equation x2 + (y + 4)2 = 25 represents circle P. Circle Q is obtained by shifting circle P up 3 units in the xy-plane. Which of the following equations represents circle Q?
rationale: The standard form (x − h)2 + (y − k)2 = r2 describes a circle with center (h, k) and radius r. Circle P, x2 + (y + 4)2 = 25, has center (0, −4) and radius 5. Shifting up 3 units adds 3 to the y-coordinate of the center, giving center (0, −1) and the equation x2 + (y + 1)2 = 25.
